name: Ciprofloxacin
description: Fluoroquinolone antibiotic with broad gram-negative coverage. Used for serious wound infections, penetrating abdominal trauma (GI flora coverage), and urinary tract infections. Available PO and IV.
contraindications: Children and adolescents < 18 years (relative -- risk of tendinopathy)
warnings: Tendon rupture risk, especially Achilles tendon. Discontinue if tendon pain develops.
May prolong QT interval -- caution with other QT-prolonging drugs.
Avoid in pregnancy if alternatives available.

pediatric.dose: 10-20 mg/kg IV q8-12h (max 400 mg/dose). PO: 10-20 mg/kg q12h (max 750 mg/dose).
pediatric.note: Use only when benefits outweigh tendinopathy risk. Reserve for serious infections.

obstetric.note: Avoid in pregnancy if alternatives available. Use only for life-threatening infections where benefit outweighs risk.

source: JTS CPG: Wound Care / Infectious Disease
